title: LangBridge: Interpreting Image as a Combination of Language Embeddings
main_contributions: Enables interpretable vision-language alignment by grounding visual representations in LLM vocab embedding
Plug-and-play design ensures efficient reuse across multiple LLMs with nearly no performance degradation
Can be directly applied to larger models while maintaining competitive performance

architecture.embedding_method: Decomposes visual features into weighted combinations of LLM's vocabulary vectors to form visual embeddings
architecture.comparison: Unlike MLP approaches that directly map visual features into LLM's text embedding space

process: Extract visual features using Vision Transformers
Transform them through stage-specific MLPs into visual embeddings
Compute cosine similarities between visual embeddings and text embeddings
Identify top semantically related text tokens for each visual embedding

key_findings: Visual embeddings demonstrate strong correlations with semantically-related text tokens
MLPs effectively map visual features into the text embedding space of LLMs
limitations_addressed: Poor understanding of how MLPs bridge the modality gap
MLP adapter requires retraining when switching LLM backbones due to input dimension mismatch

related_work.baseline_approach: Mainstream LVLMs following LLaVA architecture using MLPs
related_work.alternative_approach: Ovis method using visual embedding table to produce structural visual embeddings
